items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for shops
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help businesses get their licenses in order, particularly targeting shopping centers. This could affect local business compliance and city revenue.

items.headline: New economic incentives for retail and small businesses announced
items.summary: The city is introducing incentive programs to attract and support retail shops and startups in Cupertino. This signals an effort to strengthen the local business community and downtown areas.

items.headline: New mixed-use housing development approved for East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road. This is the second reading, meaning the project is moving forward and will bring new housing to the area.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program. If awarded, this funding could support local housing development projects and reduce barriers to building new homes.

items.headline: City seeking public input on federal housing funding priorities
items.summary: Santa Clara is holding a public hearing on March 24 to gather community feedback on how to spend HUD grant money for housing programs in the 2026/27 fiscal year. This is your chance to influence which housing initiatives get funded.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program likely addresses vehicle living and parking issues in the city, giving residents a chance to learn details and provide input.
